select_when: A strategy, offer, or position needs to communicate credibility under uncertainty, and the current message may not align with the actual underlying quality.
The real risk is that appearance, framing, or symbolic gestures are substituting for truth-aligned capability or commitment.
A team needs to make the intended action legible to others without relying on vague mission language or intellectual laziness.
The next move should clarify what behavior, proof, or costly commitment will make the message trustworthy.
avoid_when: The work is mostly impression management, and better signaling would only make a weak underlying offer look cleaner.
The team already has clear truthful proof and now mainly needs execution rather than another round of message engineering.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on costly credibility, impression-versus-reality gaps, and action-legible communication rather than generic persuasion rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor communication sprawl because the raw source is stronger on signal quality, action alignment, and anti-symbolic-substitution discipline.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that also spans framing, schema activation, and execution translation.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on signaling under ambiguity, bridging strategy to execution, and distinguishing real credibility from symbolic appearance.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are grounded in source warnings that cheap signals and impression management can hide weak underlying reality.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on credibility and signal-quality problems, while the typed input remains a compact reviewed label.

output_type.note: The source yields clearer costly signals and aligned action cues; the output label compresses that into an operational artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Signaling is strongest as systems-aware credibility diagnosis, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
